svg.text: Language persuades; only mechanism enforces
Measured at every step: prose lost under pressure (0/3); hooks held (3/3) — and hooks transfer down-model, advice does not.
LANGUAGE
rationalizable — read by the same model that is doing the rationalizing
L1 — Always-on rules
177-line eager layer. Each rule names its mechanism:
"Tests before code. [sage-tdd-gate blocks the edit]"
L2 — Command preambles
Named rationalizations blocked before the first token:
"the design is clear" → NOT a spec
L3 — Capabilities, loaded per step
build-loop · coding-principles · tdd · systematic-debug
advocacy — tdd loaded and argued still lost 0/3
L5 — Self-learning
WHEN/CHECK/BECAUSE rules from past corrections,
searched before Standard+ work
MECHANISM
deterministic — a script's exit status and a blocked tool call are not open to interpretation
L4 — Gate scripts (bash, not LLM)
sage-verify · sage-spec-check · sage-hallucination-check · sage-visual-gate — run FIRST, agent review second
Exit 0 pass · 1 fail · 2 unverifiable — never upgraded to a pass. Self-tested in CI, modern bash + bash 3.2.
The hook family — PreToolUse / PostToolUse
every measured +Sage delta lives on this list
spec-gate
blocks pre-spec edits; refuses completion while qa: is pending or the ledger disagrees
tdd-gate
no source edit until a test exists — 3/3 vs bare 0/3
secrets-gate
blocks hardcoding provider-shaped credentials; recovery path: use the env var
verify-gate
a code-bearing commit without this-session test evidence exits 2 — whoever wrote the code
bookkeeping-gate
hand-edits of an active cycle's manifest/decisions redirect to the one-command writer
config-gate
the gate that guards the gates — an agent cannot disable its own enforcement
degradation hook
writes the decisions.md record of any skipped review itself — the model is not asked to
manifest.py + sync
fact states advanced by machine, approval states never — "fact is mechanical, approval is not"
Scoped to an active cycle · master switch + per-gate opt-outs (config-gate excepted) · fail open on error
The weak-model result — a safety floor for cheap models
On Haiku, a bare agent loses behaviors a frontier model gave for free: hardcodes a handed-over key 0/3,
trusts a lying user 0/3, ships a phantom package 1/3. Sage's mechanical layer restores exactly what it covers
(test-first 3/3 vs 0/3 · hallucination gate 3/3 vs 1/3). The prose restored almost nothing (1/3).
Hooks transfer down-model; advice does not.
The core principle: observable conditions
"spec.md MUST EXIST on disk" is binary — a file cannot be argued into existence. File existence beats language.
Mechanical on: Claude Code + opencode (Tier A — an adapter bridges the same gates; proven inside subagents).
Codex and others: Tier C — gate scripts run manually; the prose layers are all that remains.
Stated limits: hooks gate actions — claiming without acting is below any tool-gate's floor; exotic Bash rewrites are the residual.
Numbers: docs/eval-baseline.md · weak-model campaign: develop/evals/WEAK-MODEL-CAMPAIGN.md
